iocs.capabilities: Keylogging, screen capture, clipboard data collection
iocs.comment: SlimAgent keylogger
BeardShell trojan
BeardShell C2 infrastructure, legit service abused by the malware
Covenant C2 infrastructure, legit service abused by the malware
BeardShell only executes in taskhost.exe or taskhostw.exe
SlimAgent only executes in explorer.exe

ttps.comment: Acquire Infrastructure: Web Services - BeardShell uses Icedrive and modified Covenant uses Filen as cloud C2 infrastructure.
Develop Capabilities: Malware - SlimAgent and BeardShell are custom malware maintained by Sednit developers.
Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell - BeardShell executes PowerShell commands in-process.
Shared Modules - Core implants are delivered as DLL modules (SlimAgent and BeardShell).
Event Triggered Execution: Component Object Model Hijacking - Persistence is achieved through COM hijacking.
Obfuscated Files or Information - BeardShell applies opaque-predicate obfuscation around token handling code.
Deobfuscate/Decode Files or Information - BeardShell decrypts protected strings and tokens at runtime.
Execution Guardrails: Environmental Keying - Implant logic is gated by specific host process conditions (taskhost/taskhostw/explorer).
Hide Artifacts: Hidden Files and Directories - SlimAgent stores spying logs in hidden files.
System Information Discovery - BeardShell fingerprints compromised hosts.
Data from Local System - BeardShell, Covenant, and SlimAgent collect local host data.
Input Capture: Keylogging - SlimAgent records keystrokes.
Screen Capture - SlimAgent captures screenshots from infected systems.
Clipboard Data - SlimAgent collects clipboard content.
Data Obfuscation: Junk Data - BeardShell wraps exfiltrated content in fake image artifacts.
Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols - HTTPS is used for cloud-mediated C2 communications.
Web Service: Bidirectional Communication - Icedrive and Filen APIs are used as command channels.
Encrypted Channel: Asymmetric Cryptography - Communications are protected via encrypted channels including RSA-protected sessions in Covenant flow.
Exfiltration Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage - Collected data is exfiltrated through cloud storage services.

queries.comment: SlimAgent and BeardShell execution, dll injection
SlimAgent 2024 file detection by specific function names. content:"FN_log_window_text"
Modified Covenant file detection by specific function names. content:"GruntUUID" and content:"filenClient"

xrefs.comment: BeardShell executes only under taskhost.exe or taskhostw.exe conditions.
SlimAgent execution is constrained to explorer.exe.
BeardShell uses Icedrive cloud URLs for command exchange.
Provisioned cloud web services are directly used for bidirectional C2.
Custom malware capability is delivered through DLL modules.
Module execution is persisted with COM hijacking.
Persistent components execute scripted PowerShell tasks.
Execution flow enforces process-based environmental keying.
Guardrail validation is followed by host fingerprinting.
System discovery informs local data collection.
Collected local context includes keyboard capture.
Keylogging activity is complemented with screenshot capture.
Visual capture is paired with clipboard collection.
Captured data is stored as hidden artifacts.
Hidden collection is packed with junk-data style obfuscation.
Obfuscated payloads are sent via encrypted channels.
Encrypted sessions are carried over HTTPS web protocols.
HTTPS communications are implemented through cloud web services.
The same cloud services are used to exfiltrate collected data.

iocs.comment: C2 server for additional payload retrieval
Commercial VPN segment used for egress to Israel targets
Israeli VPN node used for egress
Zero-trust Wireguard-based mesh network deployed for internal lateral movement
Disk encryption utility downloaded for destructive encryption
LSASS credential dumping pre-wiping. Value is keywords enumeration, cmdline is <path>rundll32.exe <paht>comsvcs.dll, MiniDump <PID> <path_filename>.dmp full
Copies registry hives from VSS (SYSTEM hive). Value is cmdline start, cmdline is wmic[.exe] process call create "reg.exe save HKLM\SYSTEM <path_filename>"

iocs.name: handala.exe
iocs.comment: Handala Wiper, dropped by initial access vector and used for destructive payload execution

ttps.comment: Trusted Relationship - IT and service providers are leveraged as access paths into downstream victim environments.
Brute Force - Repeated VPN authentication attempts are used to gain entry.
Valid Accounts: Domain Accounts - Compromised domain credentials are used for operational access.
External Remote Services - Operators access victim environments through VPN/RDP remote services.
Remote Services: Remote Desktop Protocol - Extensive hands-on lateral movement is performed with RDP.
Lateral Tool Transfer - Tooling is manually moved and staged across compromised hosts.
Ingress Tool Transfer - NetBird and VeraCrypt are downloaded and deployed on victim systems.
Protocol Tunneling - NetBird is used to tunnel operator traffic into internal network segments.
Windows Management Instrumentation - WMIC remote process creation is used during pre-impact operations.
OS Credential Dumping: LSASS Memory - rundll32 with comsvcs MiniDump is used to dump LSASS.
OS Credential Dumping: Security Account Manager - Registry hive export activity targets credential material.
Account Discovery: Domain Accounts - ADRecon enumerates Active Directory account information.
Domain Policy Modification: Group Policy Modification - Wiper components are distributed through GPO mechanisms.
Boot or Logon Initialization Scripts: Network Logon Script - Logon scripts trigger destructive components.
Scheduled Task/Job: Scheduled Task - Scheduled execution is used for staged destructive launch.
Execution Guardrails: Environmental Keying - Domain Admin validation is used before destructive actions.
Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell - AI-assisted PowerShell scripts orchestrate wiping activity.
Data Destruction - Multiple destructive routines erase files and system content.
Disk Wipe: Disk Structure Wipe - Custom MBR wiping is used for deep destructive impact.
Inhibit System Recovery - Parallel destructive methods are used to complicate restoration.
Data Encrypted for Impact - VeraCrypt encryption is used to deny system and data access.

queries.value: Process | where ProcessImageName in~('rundll32.exe', 'powershell.exe') | where ProcessCommandLine contains 'comsvcs.dll' and ProcessCommandLine contains 'MiniDump'
Process | where ProcessImageName in~('wmic.exe', 'powershell.exe') | where ProcessCommandLine contains 'process call create' and ProcessCommandLine contains 'reg.exe save'
DeviceNetworkEvents | where RemoteIP in ('107.189.19.52', '169.150.227.0/24', '146.185.219.235') | where ActionType == 'ConnectionSuccess'
queries.comment: Suspicious C2/VPN connections from Iranian actors. Here could also detect IR IPs

xrefs.comment: Detection query aligns with observed LSASS dump command behavior.
Detection query aligns with WMIC-based registry hive dumping activity.
Network query covers C2 and VPN infrastructure observed in intrusions.
Trusted-provider access patterns include repeated credential attack activity.
Brute-force and credential abuse enable use of valid domain accounts.
Compromised credentials are used over external remote services.
Initial remote access transitions into internal RDP-based movement.
RDP footholds are used to transfer operator tooling laterally.
Transferred tooling includes downloaded utilities such as NetBird and VeraCrypt.
Ingressed NetBird tooling is used to tunnel operational traffic.
Tunnel-enabled access supports remote WMI process execution.
WMI-executed commands precede LSASS credential dumping.
LSASS dumping is paired with registry/SAM extraction techniques.
Credential access supports subsequent AD account discovery.
Domain awareness is operationalized through GPO modification for distribution.
Group Policy changes deliver network logon script execution.
Logon script staging chains into scheduled-task driven payload launch.
Scheduled destructive workflows include environment-based guardrail checks.
Validated execution context enables scripted destructive orchestration.
PowerShell logic drives destructive file and system actions.
Destructive phase escalates into disk-structure wiping.
Destruction includes inhibiting practical recovery.
Destructive operations are combined with encryption-for-impact.

iocs.comment: Dropper DLL (Variant 1) filename: 2_2.d
Dropper DLL (Variant 1) filename: 2_2.d
Dropper DLL (Variant 1) filename: 2_2.d
PixyNetLoader dropper DLL (Variant 2) filename: table.d
Shellcode loader filename: EhStoreShell.dll
PNG container with embedded shellcode filename: SplashScreen.png
Malicious domain used to host payloads
Malicious domain used in campaign infrastructure
URL hosting MiniDoor dropper DLL
URL hosting PixyNetLoader
EhStoreShell.dll executes malicious logic only when loaded by explorer.exe
Scheduled task creation command used for staged execution
Attacker-controlled mailbox used by the Outlook VBA forwarding workflow

ttps.comment: Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment - Weaponized RTF lures are delivered as malicious attachments.
User Execution: Malicious File - Opening the exploit RTF is required to trigger execution flow.
Exploitation for Client Execution - CVE-2026-21509 is exploited to launch staged infection.
Office Application Startup: Add-ins - MiniDoor VBA project persists at Outlook startup.
Email Collection: Local Email Collection - Outlook VBA forwards local mailbox content to attacker-controlled mailboxes.
Scheduled Task/Job: Scheduled Task - OneDriveHealth scheduled task triggers explorer restart and staged execution.
Event Triggered Execution: Component Object Model Hijacking - CLSID InProcServer32 hijack loads malicious EhStoreShell.dll.
Obfuscated Files or Information: Steganography - Shellcode is hidden in PNG LSB data (SplashScreen.png).
Virtualization/Sandbox Evasion: Time Based Checks - Sleep timing validation is used as anti-analysis guardrail.
Execution Guardrails: Mutual Exclusion - Mutexes are used to prevent duplicate implant execution.
Obfuscated Files or Information: Dynamic API Resolution - DJB2 API hashing is used to resolve APIs at runtime.
Deobfuscate/Decode Files or Information - XOR and encoded payload material is decoded during runtime.
Native API - Native APIs are used for memory allocation and shellcode execution.
Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols - HTTPS is used for implant communications.
Web Service: Bidirectional Communication - Filen API is abused through C2Bridge for command exchange.

vulns.comment: Crafted RTF exploit used in Operation Neusploit delivery chain.

queries.value: DeviceProcessEvents | where FileName =~ 'schtasks.exe' | where ProcessCommandLine has '/Create' and ProcessCommandLine has 'OneDriveHealth' and ProcessCommandLine has 'office.xml'
queries.comment: Detect scheduled task creation observed in PixyNetLoader chain
Detect network activity to campaign infrastructure

queries.value: rule APT28_Operation_Neusploit_PixyNetLoader { strings: $m1 = "asagdugughi41" ascii $m2 = "OneDriveHealth" ascii $m3 = "EhStoreShell.dll" ascii condition: 2 of ($m*) }
queries.comment: Static detections based on strings explicitly documented in the report

xrefs.comment: Operation Neusploit chain: exploit RTF -> dropper DLL -> persistence via COM hijack and scheduled task -> shellcode loader -> Covenant Grunt C2
Spearphishing attachment triggers exploit execution.
Variant 1 establishes Outlook add-in startup behavior.
Outlook add-in persistence enables local mailbox collection and forwarding.
Variant 2 sets scheduled task based staged execution.
Scheduled execution supports COM hijacking persistence.
Persistent loader retrieves steganographic payload content.
Stego payload execution includes sandbox timing checks.
Timing-based checks chain into mutex-based execution control.
Execution guardrails protect dynamically resolved API execution path.
Dynamically resolved routines decode staged payload data.
Decoded payloads are executed via native API calls.
In-memory shellcode execution leads to active web-protocol C2.
HTTPS C2 is implemented over bidirectional web service.

ttps.comment: Adversary-in-the-Middle - Gateway and edge implants intercept and modify victim traffic in-line.
Network Sniffing - Deep packet inspection monitors and parses routed traffic for targeting and telemetry.
Data Manipulation: Transmitted Data Manipulation - HTTP/DNS responses are altered to redirect victims and serve malicious payloads.
Ingress Tool Transfer - DKnife update and delivery components fetch malicious archives and payload files from C2 infrastructure.
Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols - C2 and telemetry use HTTP/HTTPS channels.
Protocol Tunneling - N2N-based remote component establishes tunneled communications for remote control and relay.
Exfiltration Over C2 Channel - Captured user/network telemetry and credentials are exfiltrated through C2 endpoints.

xrefs.comment: Core DKnife binaries implement AiTM interception and packet inspection capabilities.
Downloaded install and payload components represent inbound tool transfer.
Redirected update package delivery URL is aligned with response manipulation behavior.
C2 infrastructure and protocol endpoints support web-protocol C2 and data exfiltration.
Inline AiTM interception enables direct manipulation of transmitted content.
Manipulated traffic redirects victims to malware delivery workflows.
Delivered tooling continues operations over web-based command channels.
Web C2 operations are augmented with tunnel-based overlay transport.
Tunneled control channel supports sustained telemetry and credential exfiltration.

iocs.comment: StoatWaffle IOC listed by NTT Security Japan
StoatWaffle IOC listed by NTT Security Japan
StoatWaffle IOC listed by NTT Security Japan
StoatWaffle IOC listed by NTT Security Japan
StoatWaffle IOC listed by NTT Security Japan
Malicious VS Code task configuration executed on folder open
Batch downloader launched from the initial task chain
First-stage StoatWaffle Node.js downloader
Downloaded Vercel-hosted payloads are executed through cmd.exe
First-stage downloader polling path on StoatWaffle C2
Second-stage downloader polling path on StoatWaffle C2
Credential theft upload path
Installed software inventory upload path
RAT command retrieval path
RAT command result upload path

ttps.comment: User Execution: Malicious File - Opening and trusting a malicious VS Code repository triggers task execution.
Command and Scripting Interpreter: Windows Command Shell - Initial Vercel-delivered stages execute through cmd.exe.
Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript - Loader, stealer, and RAT logic execute as Node.js JavaScript code.
Ingress Tool Transfer - Multi-stage downloader fetches additional components (vscode-bootstrap.cmd, env.npl, modules).
Credentials from Password Stores: Credentials from Web Browsers - Browser credentials and extension data are stolen.
Software Discovery - Installed software inventory is collected from the victim endpoint.
Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols - C2 polling and command exchanges use HTTP API paths.
Exfiltration Over C2 Channel - Stolen data and RAT command results are uploaded over the same control channel.

queries.comment: Detect outbound connections to StoatWaffle IOC IP addresses

queries.value: DeviceProcessEvents | where FileName in~ ('cmd.exe','node.exe') | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ('vscode-bootstrap.cmd','env.npl')
queries.comment: Detect VS Code / Node.js execution chain associated with StoatWaffle

xrefs.comment: Malicious tasks.json triggers the staged downloader chain.
The VS Code, command shell, and Node.js stages map to user execution and staged payload retrieval.
HTTP API paths support C2 polling, command execution, and result exfiltration.
Upload paths are used for credential theft output, software discovery output, and exfiltration.
Repository opening and trust lead to staged downloader retrieval.
Initial shell execution transitions into Node.js-based scripted execution.
Node.js stagers pull and launch additional payload modules.
Downloaded stealer modules perform browser credential theft.
Credential theft stage is followed by installed-software enumeration.
Collected host data is sent through HTTP-based C2 API communications.
C2 protocol flow carries exfiltration of stolen data and RAT output.
